_template: world-seed v2.0 — fill this in before initializing a new root. See ADR-038.
_usage: Copy to runs/{rootId}/world-seed.json when creating a new root. Remove all _guide and _template fields before committing the live version.
_supersedes: world v1.0 (runs/root_2026_04_14/world-seed.json — retroactive declaration, kept as historical record per ADR-029) and v1.1 (template-only, never used by a live root). v2.0 introduces anthropomorphic society, NE-US regional anchor, time-compressed seasonal arc, and unique-item inventory.

world_kind._guide: World-level register and invariants. The pipeline reads these as constraints on every artifact.
world_kind.tone_anchor: _guide: Reference for the social register. Default: 'Daniel Tiger with animal tendencies — neighbors talk, reason, barter, hold grudges, but retain instincts (alertness, food drives, territory). Non-violent.'
world_kind.violence_rule: _guide: Default: 'Physical contact is emotional display, not harm. No one gets hurt. Conflict resolves through words, trade, or retreat.'
world_kind.speech_rule: _guide: Default: 'All named animals can speak and reason. Instinct colors voice — a hungry squirrel speaks tersely; a startled bird speaks too fast — but does not block speech.'

geography._guide: Persistent named places + the regional anchor. The anchor constrains weather and biome; place names accumulate meaning over the month. Future extrapolations of geography should remain within the anchor region.
geography.region_anchor: _guide: Real-world region whose patterns the world borrows. Default: 'Northeast US — temperate deciduous, mixed hardwood forest, four-season, fieldstone walls, ferny understory.'

home_base._guide: YY's start/end point each day. May carry forward from a prior root.

named_places._guide: One entry per named place. 4-6 places at root start.
named_places.type: _guide: cache_site | path | boundary | social_node | shelter | water | meadow | unknown

society._guide: Capability rules + named neighbors. Replaces v1's social_field — neighbors are first-class.
society.capability: _guide: Default: 'Neighbors speak, reason, hold relationships and slow grudges. Trades are remembered. Animal instincts persist but do not block social behavior.'
customs: _guide: Norms that shape interactions. E.g. 'Fair trades are expected; uneven trades are remembered.'

neighbors._guide: One entry per recurring named neighbor. 2-4 at root start.
neighbors.role: _guide: forager | trader | builder | info_broker | host | guard | etc.
neighbors.disposition: _guide: warm | cautious | chatty | reserved | opportunistic | enthusiastic

economy._guide: How wealth and exchange work. Loose — described qualitatively, not stat-tracked. Bulk currency is narrated; unique items are tracked via inventory_catalog.

examples: _guide: acorns, pinecones, berries, hazelnuts — bulk and perishable
_guide: bark strips, pine resin, moss bundles, shed feathers, smooth stones — rarer, store-of-value

economy.exchange_norms: _guide: Default: 'Barter-based. Fair trades are the norm; uneven trades are remembered. Prices drift with season and scarcity.'
economy.advancement: _guide: Default: 'Foraging and exploring improve standing. Finding rare materials or productive locations creates wealth. Hoarding without trade is socially flat.'

mobility._guide: Movement constraints — affects geography (distances matter) and economy (travel costs).
mobility.method: _guide: Default: 'Physical exertion only. Paws, claws, tails. No vehicles, no machinery.'
mobility.constraints: _guide: Default: 'Travel cost is real — long trips spend food and attention. Distances are felt.'

seasonal_arc._guide: 30 run-days = 1 in-world calendar year. Seasons shift visibly. The pipeline reads the schedule to know which season each day falls in.
seasonal_arc.compression: _guide: Default: '30 run-days ≈ Jan→Dec, weighted toward shoulder seasons (spring/summer/autumn = 22 days; winter framing = 8 days).'
seasonal_arc.weather_register: _guide: Default for NE anchor: 'Northeast US temperate — mud season, late frosts, leaf-out, summer thunderstorms, fall foliage, first snow.'

schedule._guide: One entry per season-segment. Day ranges are inclusive. Adjust to taste but cover days 1-30.

inventory_catalog._guide: World v2.0 introduces unique items. Each is a singular object; holding it changes social interactions. Items appear through events (found, given, traded). Catalog seeds the world's vocabulary; additional items may emerge over the month.

items._guide: One entry per unique item. id is referenced in branch starting_inventory and in event payloads.
interaction_unlocks: _guide: Specific options this item enables. E.g. 'trade with Mira for gathered_materials'.

starting_conditions._guide: YY's Day 1 state. food/health/attention reflect seasonal_arc honestly — late-winter starts are leaner than autumn ones.
active_burdens: _guide: Soft burdens carried into Day 1. E.g. 'winter_thinning' resolves when food crosses 0.5.

starting_inventory._guide: Per-branch starting inventory using item ids from inventory_catalog. Lists may differ between branches — this is one mechanism for branch divergence.

identity_notes: _guide: 1-2 notes about where YY is psychologically at root start.

atmosphere._guide: Texture as narrative resource. Used for event translation choices.

authorial_intent._guide: What you want to track. Direction, not plot.
authorial_intent.branch_hypothesis: _guide: Name the divergence the alt branch is exploring. E.g. 'alt1 starts holding a status object — does a single inherited item reshape 30 days of social positioning?'
